solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    vector<int> maxDepthAfterSplit(string seq) {
        vector<int> result;
        int depth = 0;
        for (char c : seq) {
            if (c == '(') {
                depth++;
                result.push_back(depth % 2);
            } else {
                result.push_back(depth % 2);
                depth--;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution assigns each character to either subsequence A or B based on the current depth modulo 2. This ensures balanced nesting depth between the two subsequences, minimizing the maximum depth. The approach is efficient and uses linear time and space.
